text: Hands up, how many of you fell for the fake remake post? I’m in bed with a cold and have nothing better to do right now, so I was wondering about this. To list just a few of the many red flags, no screenshots of new features, crazy feature scope, no prior experience of rom hacking, and admits to not even having played the game they’re supposedly working on. Anyway, considering the unlikely amount of traction the post got and the size of this sub, I was curious how many of the other commenters there are even real? People aren’t normally this gullible, but the classic psychological trick is to fill the room with confederates and you can get the outnumbered participant to believe the sky is green if everyone else is saying it. text: Would like help with the first "Unbound" playthrough. What would I like help with? Picking move sets for my team and am open to better team suggestions in terms of best or most powerful with best synergy etc. (Because there are too many damn moves to consider and I'm curious what the best move set might be for my team). Would also like to know the suggestions behind the move sets being suggested. (I'm okay with story spoilers, I'm just here more for the gameplay) Quick background: Playing a bit of Alpha Sapphire right now and thinking of stopping, maybe not my thing? I looked into ROM hacks for the first time and settled in on Unbound. Mostly for fun with a decent enough challenge without going full sweet mode. (Already completed Yellow, Platinum, Heartgold, Crystal, Black and Black 2 within the past year and mostly enjoyed it) I have not settled on a difficulty, I've never bothered with EVs, I'm kinda more just for rule of cool in a way based on my personal restrictions. Maybe light EVs might be fine or if doing EV is too difficult or boring? So I think I'd either chose "Difficult" or maybe "Expert" difficulty as the most likely. Maybe "Difficult." Personal restrictions: (They're more thematic restrictions than anything else) Only gen 1 and 2 Pokémon (does not apply to starters) - I just liked the look of them the most Any Eevee evolution is allowed (regardless of generation) No legendary Pokémon Mega evolutions allowed No pseudo legendary spam (so no half the team being Metagross, Tyranitar and Garchomp in the same team) The team I'm considering so far: (I am pretty set on the team below, but I am open to suggestions for changing the team based on personal restrictions above) Metagross (Main mega) Gyarados Crobat Espeon Lapras Arcanine EDIT: (After reading a few replies) I think I'mma stick to "Difficult" difficulty settings. text: 900 streak in Battle Tower (Standard Single Format). I'm close to 1000 streak again (I said again because I reached 1000 streak before but I got disqualified because I wasn't able to save before leaving the game and my save state was so far back so I just prefer restarting over) I used all my favorite Pokemon to win especially Mega Salamence is my No. 1 favorite Pokemon of all time and my ace Pokemon And also this is still not my final team since I am still deciding what my dream team actually is if I was in a Pokemon world all I know is that Salamence and Garchomp is a must in this team since they are my top favorite In short I'm using my team of favorites lmao There are times I use Forretress since its also my favorite Pokemon and was an amazing hazard setter But if I'm going to give you a tip in Battle Frontier use Prankster Pokemon with Taunt because majority of the time the opponents lead is either setting stats boost or hazard set up I use my favorite Gen 3 Pokemon Sableye to cripple physical attacker with Will-O-Wisp after Taunting them, I use Thundurus for my 2nd Prankster Pokemon because crippling their speed with Thunder Wave is a game changer and as well as a priority defog. But Blaziken Swords Dance and Speed Boost Baton Pass carries my streak just imagine how scary passing a +2 Physical Attack and Speed on Mega Salamence, the moment you succeed on those strat its basically game over unless you face a prankster user with Will-O-Wisp or Thunder Wave or Pokemon with Unaware but even then it still hit like a truck I got Garchomp as my fast stealth rock Pokemon though I have Foretress before as my main hazard setter I'm still undecided whether to switch it with Alolan Ninetales. But regardless Garchomp is kinda my second sweeper it is also absolutely monstrous even after 1 swords dance and if you managed to succeed with scale shot speed boost then it's game over unless your opponent has air balloon or levitate I also run Groundium Z on him, but most of the time I just use him to set up stealth rocks.

text: Third Time's The Charm (An Unbound Nuzlocke). So for those of you who're new here, hi! I've done this before. Granted, I've never attempted this in the completed 2.1.1.1 update, but all the same, I've attempted this Nuzlocke twice in the past. Both attempts are documented on my account, with some even being, perhaps, decent quality posts! Both attempts were performed on the Difficult in-game difficulty, both followed the same rules (detailed below, as this run will follow them as well), and both ended in unfortunate fashions; the first run died at the hands of Marlon atop Crystal Peak, the second died due to a corrupted save file at the steps of the Elite Four. Frankly, when my save file got corrupted just before the Elite Four, I gave up. I figured that was divine intervention trying to tell me to step away. It's been about 3 years, and I've got some free time right now, so I'm going back in. This is my third and final attempt at nuzlocking Pokémon Unbound! This is for all the marbles; this is to set the record straight; this is to prove, once and for all, that it can be done, and that I can do it. The rules are as follows: I can only catch/obtain 1 Pokémon per route, and it must be the first Pokémon I encounter. Everyone gets nicknames! Suggestions are welcome! If a Pokémon faints, it's placed in a PC box titled "Grave" and cannot be used for the remainder of the run. No over-leveling the game's hard level caps for capped EXP share. No using items in battle except held items. The battle mode will remain on Set for the entire run. Dupes Clause: I cannot catch the same species or evolution line as a Pokémon I already have, and must instead reroll the encounter. If no unique encounters exist on a route....sucks to be me! Shiny Clause: I can catch any shiny Pokémon I find, but cannot use it unless I already possess and choose to replace a non-shiny version of it. So a shiny Aron can only be used if I already have an Aron, and using the shiny would make me retire the non-shiny to the "Grave" box. If I white out, I reset the run and try again! We don't quit until the Elite Four is done! Difficult is set to Difficult for the whole attempt. I plan to update on my progress every Tuesday following this post. I hope y'all stick around to check in on the journey! The starter at the beginning of this run is Larvitar. That's right: I chose hard mode. Let's do this! Thanks to Maxlabbe and Ajsansr201121 for their encouragement and advice in previous attempts. I strive to make this a finale you can enjoy.
label: discussion
notes: Could be achievement but the User is saying that they plan to do a challenge, not showcasing their achivements.

text: Finally finished Unbound. Registered all 905 Pokémon, got every unique gimmick item in the game, and got the Black Card. I finally did it I finished Pokémon Unbound on Difficult mode. If there was a PS trophies or Steam achievements thing in Unbound, I legit think that I would have them all. Or at least most of it if we're not including the other game modes like Expert and Insane. But I just feel so tired from playing this game for so long that I can't bring myself to play a New Game+ on a higher difficulty. Everything I've done to finish Unbound includes: Getting 5 stars on the Trainer Card. Registered all 905 Pokémon in the Pokédex. Collecting every Pokémon in the game, including all Pokémon with obvious gender differences, both original and regional variants of Pokémon with a regional variants, Pokémon with unique forms based on environment or nature, all Pikachus, Spiky Eared Pichu, all Unowns, all Rotoms, all Vivillons, all Furfrous, all Miniors, all Alcremies, every GMax form, and both Kyurem and Necrozma's fused forms. Collected every unique gimmick item in the game like every Mega Stone, every Z-Crystal, every Arc Plate, every Memory Disc, every Genesect Drive, both Blue and Red Orbs, both Rusted Sword and Rusted Shield, all of the Creation Trio Orbs, Gracidea, Reveal Glass, DNA Splicers, Prison Bottle, N-Solarizer, and N-Lunarizer. Finished every Gym Leader Shiny Pokémon trade in Magnolia Cafe. The only thing that absolutely sucks about doing these was getting the Genesect Shock Drive and Chill Drive, as far as I know, there's absolutely no way to get these 2 drives in-game or in the Battle Frontier Demo, you have to trade it over from completely different Pokémon rom hacks that are compatible with Unbound Cloud or get it from someone else who already have those drives. So I admittedly had to get those the unfavorable way, since I didn't wanna play some new rom hack just to pass those 2 drives over to an almost finished game. But aside from that, items like Incinium Z, Rusted Sword/Shield, Burn Drive, and Douse Drive were all obtained from Random Battles in the Battle Tower. There were also some Pokémon that for the life of me would never appear in Random Battles no matter how many times I rerolled, so I ended up just getting them from the Battle Frontier Demo's front desk. The two Pokémon I'm almost confident never appears in Random Battles are Kubfu and base form Calyrex; the Pokémon I know can appear in Random Battles, but took so long to appear that I just got it from the Demo are the other Cosplay Pikachus, except Libre and Belle. And since I was collecting Galar Legendaries in the Battle Tower, I figured I might as well get the fused forms of Kyurem and Necrozma as well too so that the original ones I caught don't have to be fused together.
label: achievements

text: Mel left a bad taste,thoughts? WHOMP, this got long, sorry! First off, I want to be clear that this is not a criticism of the game, the issue I’m about to bring up is something that the game LITERALLY asked you about right in the beginning when deciding what difficulty is best for you. So I’m not knocking it, just curious of all your thoughts. Playing on Expert. Between the third and the fourth gym, I spent a lot of time catching and breeding different Pokémon. I wasn’t tied to 6, I was cycling a few out to, but had a solid 4 with good synergy. Lots of breeding, Dex nav and Dresco battle house. No complaints, I enjoy building teams. I know all of the gyms are a puzzle with a gimmick. I get to Mel. My team gets their ass kicked due to the inverse. Right away I think to myself, “Ok it seems like the OBVIOUS answer to this one is toxic spikes, gengar, hex” But I always try to go against the grain a little bit. Wanted to stick somewhere close to the team composition I worked hard to build. Tried a bunch of different compositions and keep getting smacked. “Ok, now I’m starting to think one of the ONLY answers to this one is toxic spikes, gengar, hex” Try some more, get super frustrated and then give into toxic spikes, gengar, hex. Sweep Mel. On one hand, I was able to figure out the solution to his gimmick pretty quickly. But on the other, I was frustrated feeling like I was pigeon holed into that ONE solution (I’m SURE there are more). Felt hollow. I’m NOWHERE NEAR as high a level of player as many people here. I LOVE the difficulty, but I also like having the freedom to many solutions to a difficult puzzle, I didn’t feel this way with radical red. Even though the game asked me in the beginning and my answer was yes. Part of me wonders what the point of spending time to team build is, if I’m going to blow it up for one of the few solutions to each gym. Anyone else feel this way? The obvious answer is GET BETTER, I know LOL More curious if anyone else had these thoughts/frustrations!

text: First Playthrough Finished! My first romhack ever and what I imagine will stay up there as my favorite. I wanted to add an extra challenge to the game as I was worried Metagross would carry too hard with a monosteel run and boy was I wrong. Mono-steel proved harder than expected, especially in some early game gyms (the normal gym seemed engineered to hard-counter steel). The amount of fire coverage across the board meant having to pivot around a lot of one-shots and praying on Heatwave misses in double battles. Ferrothorn actually ended up being MVP, not Metagross, hard-carrying certain sections of the mid-late game. An absolute blast! Wouldn't have done it any other way
label: achievements

text: Series of bugs/glitches- save file repairable? Hi all, I’m currently trekking through NG+, with around 100 hours across the two saves, and have encountered a really unfortunate series of bugs. I’m hoping that some of the gurus on here might know how to fix it. *Bug 1: Bad Eggs* At some point one of my PC boxes got mostly filled (maybe ~20 slots) with Bad Eggs. No clue when exactly this happened, as it went into Box 20 and I was fairly early in the game, so never ventured that far into my PC. I simply got rid of the Bad Eggs using the Daycare Man, and everything progressed normally for maybe 10 more hours. *Bug 2: ??????? Items* Maybe around an hour of game time ago, I noticed I had 22 ???????? items in the general storage of my cube. Again, I simply tossed them and carried on, hoping for the best. Everything continued playing as per usual. *Bug 3: MissingNo and Encounter Freezes* I ran into grass, and encountered what I can only describe as a MissingNo type Pokemon. Question mark icon and glitched name display. The game crashed immediately after the encounter loaded. I haven’t been able to replicate this, as on subsequent attempts the game has simply frozen (sometimes with distorted audio) when I do try initiate an encounter. *Bug 4: TM case TM000* Concurrently, the majority of my TM case seems to be full of glitched TMs. Interacting with them turns the screen green and pink, and crashes/freezes the game. After scouring this subreddit, it seems that many people have encountered these bugs (usually in isolation, mind you - none have described a progression of bugs like me). Is there a solution? I’m not too keen on abandoning my NG+ file completely, but the earliest backup I have retained still has the Bad Eggs, so just rolling back isn’t an option unfortunately. Any ideas as to a solution that doesn’t involve me kissing goodbye to 100 hours of progress? **Technical Context** Emulator: Manic EMU, iOS. Using the mGBA core. Unbound Version: 2.1.1.1, correctly patched onto the Squirrels ROM. Other Info that Might Be Relevant: * I haven’t used any cheats. For whatever reason, users on other threads reporting similar issues seem insistent on blaming the OP for using cheats and corrupting their game. This isn’t the case here. * I do love using the fast-forward feature, and often run at 2x-4x speed. Hasn’t seemed to cause issues till now? * I’ve never tinkered with the clock on my device. * The game fairly regularly tells me my save file has been corrupted, and did this throughout my entire NG playthrough too. I’d learned to think nothing of it, as the game seemed to simply revert me to an earlier save where things were fine. I got in the habit of saving 2-3 times on each occasion, as this seemed to limit the loss of progress considerably. * I can post screenshots/videos of Bugs 3 & 4 if it would be helpful - just let me know. EDIT: I’ve come to conclusion that my save file is likely cooked beyond repair, though I’m not too keen on doing a full NG run again to get to where I am. Does anyone have a post-game file that doesn’t have the black trainer card yet, but does have all key items (sans perfected stat scanner?) and upgraded power items? If yes, I’d love if you could DM me to share - will be heartbreaking to see my save go, but at least I’ll only be losing ~4 gyms’ worth of NG+ progress this way.
label: help_request

text: Beat the game for the firsy time! This Rom hack was honestly some of the most fun I've had with pokemon in a long while. I actually tried to get into unbound a few times but got bored before I got to the first gym. Idk what past me was thinking because this game was straight fire! There's so many things I loved about this game that often don't happen in even official pokemon games, for example the biomes throughout the region flow nicely into each other where as say for example in hoenn there's just suddenly a desert in the corner of the map with 0 build up. My favorite area has to be blizzard city, the vibe and atmosphere is beautiful and I loved the music in the city. For the main team I used I tried to recreate my pokemon OCs team, the only difference is she has Volcarona instead of Garchomp but you don't get access to Volcarona until the post game. The game was also actually surprisingly difficult, I never got overly stuck but certain gyms and the E4 took me a few attempts. I also really appreciate the level curve in this game. In official pokemon games sometimes it feels like you're grinding for hours but here I never once had to grind and my mons were perfectly leveled for each gym. Overall I loved this hack and would love to see more like it

text: Unbound Appreciation Post. Not sure if Skeli and their team checks this, but I just wanted to express my sincere gratitude for making this game. I've been playing Pokemon games since I was 11 when red and blue came out. Having played nearly every single thing that Game Freak has put out in this franchise, I've become I guess frustrated, for lack for lack of a better word, with the direction of the franchise, and I'm definitely someone that cherishes the gameplay and feel of the pre gen-6 games. I feel like I've played heart gold, platinum, black, and black 2 to the point where there isn't anything new to experience in them anymore and have been longing for a new and similar adventure. I recently discovered roms and everyone I've spoken to told me to give unbound a try. You have taken everything that I love about those pokemon games and made it into this all encompassing and refreshing experience. It really is the exact pokemon game that I've always wanted to play. I'm currenlty 40 hours in and I haven't even made it to the 5th gym yet. I'm loving every minute and can't wait to see what's next. So again, hats off to you. Thank you for making this game.

text: First Run Complete, here's mu take on T-Tar Sand [Difficult/Vanilla...]. Best Pokemon experience in my life, couldn’t put it down, very excited to play it again. The only reason I did not complete the game strictly on Difficult is that I chose to lower the difficulty on one (1) room of the Black Ferrothorn HQ raid. The room with the motorcycle guy on turbo patrolling the hallway, and you had to duck in the sides and not be stopped? I just could not coordinate myself well enough to get the timing of running behind the plants before he turned around. It was a moment of weakness. I love my team and I’m proud of it: Tyranitar: I was randomly bombarded with Gen III OU content before I started my play through, and immediately knew upon seeing the starters that I was going for a Dragon Dance T-Tar. [Insert Rock Move] and [Insert Dark Move] were a mainstay. I fluctuated on my third attack move for a while. Early game I ran ground moves and eventually [Fire Punch] for a while. Then I got it in my head that I had to have perfect offensive coverage, so he was rocking [Brick Break] for a time, out of self-imposed necessity. Considered running 2 mins with [Earthquake]? Then got [Dragon Claw] and used that for E4 instead. Excadrill: blah blah blah Excadrill was pretty great and available early so the dream was always there. [X-Scissor] was often pretty good! Sigilyph: Pet mon and great role player. 1 of 2 magic guard users + life orb users on the team. Doesn’t take sand damage, kills fighting and ground and grass types which wreck me, F U Gliscor, Ice Beam 4 Life, doesn’t die to excadrill’s [Earthquake], and is pretty fast. Clefable: Magic Guard + Life Orb is great, + great coverage, decent bulk, doesn’t take sand damage, can heal with natural egg move (!) Soft Boiled. Also [Calm Mind] was rarely ever necessary but I tried it. Great fighting check which the sand core is vulnerable to. Gengar: Purple Evil Round Guy opposite to Pink Good Round guy. Gave him Safety Goggles just so no one on my team took damage to sand but there were probably more optimal items. Wasn’t always useful early but was very good when it was time to shine. [Focus Blast] on him was a good 3rd attack and helped fill that coverage slot without having run [Brick Break] on Tyranitar. Poison STAB was helpful for the ever present fairy types, and he usually moved first in the ghost mirror. Also another fighting and ground check. Ferrothorn: Last team member and definitely a calculated choice. I needed a water resist and grass coverage. I had two pseudo special walls in Tyranitar and Clefable, so this was a nice physically bulky mon to fill a gap. Leftovers is nice, leftovers + leech seed was fun but rarely did I have the need to actually click leech seed. Double Steel coverage was also helpful with the fairy types. Probably my least used mon but I still love him. Halfway through the game I wished I had chosen Expert or even Insane difficulty. I think Expert’s endgame would have been more engaging for me, inasmuch as I did not struggle with the last 4 gym leaders or with the E4. To be fair I did grind for perfect EVs and Coins for Orb so really I think I just made a bad call. Definitely interested in playing it again, maybe on Insane? Curious about other’s opinions on the insane experience. Please feel free to recommend other ROMhacks you’ve enjoyed anywhere near as much as you did this one.
label: achievements
notes: Could be discussion because the user is discussing the team he used to beat the Elite Four, but the post purpose is to showcase the User's first run being completed.

text: Finished my second run (on my first save that I managed to recover). Clefable is OP as always. ;[Expert][Challenging]. I had lost this save earlier so I played my first full run on sandbox. After recovering my save I completed my first legit run. I basically the same team after rushing the first two gyms to get the dexnav. The few changes I made because of late access to some Mons: 1)I had the other Gastrodon at first but I like this one better. 2)Talon was Arcanine at first because you get access to talon till route 12. 3) Upgraded to better iv versions(breeding) on everyone except ttar and serp. As for the team building, I just made a regular gen6 ou sand team structure except its Serp and Gastrodon over Amoongus and Keldeo Got pokerus but only after I had finished training everyone. Not that it matters with level 10 macho brace. It's still good for the post game. Got really lucky with the Serp ivs. 4 perfect ivs on the 10th reset. I just bottle capped defense fro 24 to 31 to get Hidden Power Ice. TTar is golden bottle capped. This team is pretty strong. The only challenging boss was Big Mo (Gym 6). I didn't realize that gale wings wouldn't work so there so I had to kill Hariyama with rocky helmet+sand+burn chip. E4 was super easy. Clef beats 3 of them. Only reset was Mole Man cause of double rock slide miss. Jax is a complete jobber and a disappointing champion, even in my second run. I think I am going to rush NG+ and play on insane. Are there any downsides to this as compared to a fresh insane save?
label: achievements
